title: Stalactite Watch
desc: The ceiling over Lava Pond sheds rock without warning. Someone has to notice before it lands.

steps.text:
Lava Pond would be dangerous enough if the trouble stayed below your feet. It does not. The chamber ceiling hangs with giant teeth of stone, all of them heat-cracked, all of them one bad tremor away from becoming everyone else's problem. Every shift includes a watcher whose job is to stare upward until something looks ready to fall.

Today's watcher has a ledger board, a copper listening rod, and the weary eyes of someone who has spent too long measuring the mood of rocks. He hands you the rod. "You can log the upper cracks from the lookout shelf," he says, "or go along the side ledge and knock down the loose shards before they join the lava by surprise. I'd prefer both. I'm only staffed for one."

High overhead, one thin line of dust trickles down from a hanging spur and vanishes in the heat haze.

options.label: Climb to the lookout shelf and log the active cracks
Walk the side ledge and clear the loose shards

steps.text:
From the lookout shelf, the whole ceiling turns into a map of bad possibilities. The listening rod helps: press it to the stone support and the structure answers in tiny vibrations, telling you which cracks are old, which are widening, and which are carrying the faint rasp of fresh movement. You mark each active seam on the ledger with charcoal strokes and severity symbols the maintenance crews will understand at a glance.

One formation above the west ledge gives a different answer than the rest - a hollow, unstable chatter that means internal separation. You mark it for immediate rope-off and signal the watcher below. He does not look pleased, but he does look ready, which matters more.

By the end of the round, three new hazards are logged, one old hazard is downgraded, and a shelf that looked safe from below is now very clearly off-limits. The watcher glances over the completed board and grunts. "Good. Nothing fell while we were looking at it. That's as close to cooperation as this ceiling gets."

options.label: Carry the ledger down before the next tremor

steps.text:
The side ledge is narrow, hot, and scattered with the sort of half-broken stone that exists only to become fully broken at inconvenient moments. You work with a hooked pole, testing each suspect shard before levering it free and shoving it outward where it can fall into the pond now instead of onto a worker later.

Most of the pieces come away cleanly. One does not. A long, jagged fragment catches, holds for a second, then snaps loose with enough force to drag the hook from your hands before tumbling into the lava below. It lands in a burst of sparks and a hiss like the pond objecting to being fed pebbles.

After that you move more carefully. By the time you finish the ledge, the obvious loose pieces are gone and the walking path feels a fraction less murderous than it did an hour ago. The watcher inspects your cleared section, then the lava where the broken stone now sinks. "Good work," he says. "Preventive death is the best kind."

options.label: Recover the hook and mark the ledge clear
